pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 61: malicious_ip = '198.51.100.42' (hardcoded in task setup script); also appears in task.json description
setup_task.sh line 60: log_file = '/var/log/aerobridge_server.log' (hardcoded in task setup script); also referenced in task_utils.sh and environment setup scripts
export_result.sh line 17: REPORT_PATH="/home/ga/Documents/incident_report.txt" (hardcoded in task export script); also in task.json description
task_utils.sh line 9: AEROBRIDGE_ADMIN_URL="http://localhost:8000/admin/" (environment-wide constant); setup_task.sh line 123 uses this URL; verified across all environment scripts

privileged_info_summary: The task simulates a credential stuffing attack from malicious IP 198.51.100.42 that successfully compromises one of three operational user accounts (ops_manager, flight_lead, or logistics_coord). The attack evidence appears in server logs at /var/log/aerobridge_server.log. The agent must access the Aerobridge administration panel at http://localhost:8000/admin/, identify the compromised user by analyzing the logs, deactivate only that user account without affecting others, and create an incident report at /home/ga/Documents/incident_report.txt. All data is synthetically generated during task setup, with the victim user randomly selected at runtime from the available operational users.
